title: OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory
app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905)
spl:
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:link"
| stats dc(src_ip) as sources, dc(dst_ip) as destinations, sum(bps_out) as total_bps by protocols, zone
| sort -sources
| table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
description: OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network ‚Äî Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.
value: OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network ‚Äî Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.
grandmaExplanation: OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network ‚Äî our sensors, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, our sensors, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more ‚Äî so faults, tampering, and safety issues are spotted right away.
implementation: Both platforms identify all industrial protocols in use via DPI. Analyze data to build protocol inventory per site/zone. Identify unexpected protocols (e.g., BACnet in a power substation, or Modbus in an enterprise zone). Compare protocol usage across sites for standardization. Feed into protocol-specific security policy development.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:activity` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:link`, `sourcetype=nozomi:session`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Both platforms identify all industrial protocols in use via DPI. Analyze data to build protocol inventory per site/zone. Identify unexpected protocols (e.g., BACnet in a power substation, or Modbus in an enterprise zone). Compare protocol usage across sites for standardization. Feed into protocol-specific security policy development.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:link"
| stats dc(src_ip) as sources, dc(dst_ip) as destinations, sum(bps_out) as total_bps by protocols, zone
| sort -sources
| table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory** ‚Äî OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network ‚Äî Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:activity` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:link`, `sourcetype=nozomi:session`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:link. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:link". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by protocols, zone** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory**): table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory** ‚Äî OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network ‚Äî Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:activity` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:link`, `sourcetype=nozomi:session`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Protocol distribution pie chart per site; protocol comparison across sites; unexpected protocol alert table; protocol trend over time.
visualization: Protocol distribution pie chart per site; protocol comparison across sites; unexpected protocol alert table; protocol trend over time.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
